column1: Workers produced much more, but typical workers' pay lagged far behind: Disconnect between productivity and typical worker's compensation, 1948–2013

Note: Data are for compensation (wages and benefits) of production/nonsupervisory workers in the private sector and net productivity of the total economy. Net productivity" is the growth of output of goods and services less depreciation per hour worked. "
Source: EPI analysis of Bureau of Labor Statistics and Bureau of Economic Analysis data Updated from Figure A in Raising America’s Pay: Why It’s Our Central Economic Policy Challenge
